texto: It is impossible for any number which is a power greater than the second to be written as a sum of two like powers. I have a truly marvelous demonstration of this proposition which this margin is too narrow to contain.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pierre de Fermat: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “It is impossible for any number which is a power greater than the second to be written as a sum o…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, impossible, number pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de number e two.

frase_original: It is impossible for any number which is a power greater than the second to be written as a sum of two like powers. I have a truly marvelous demonstration of this proposition which this margin is too narrow to contain.

texto: But it is impossible to divide a cube into two cubes, or a fourth power into fourth powers, or generally any power beyond the square into like powers; of this I have found a remarkable demonstration. This margin is too narrow to contain it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pierre de Fermat: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “But it is impossible to divide a cube into two cubes, or a fourth power into fourth powers, or ge…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, impossible, two pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de two e like.
